commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4398afea00403be5ef8e857223cfd7eef4f92162)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a delegated `ascii-box` provider with config/env/flags, archive sync, run/warmup/list/status/stop support, registration, docs, changelog text, and provider tests modeled on `upstash-box`.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the client calls `/api/box`, while live unauthenticated checks return 404 there and 401 at `/api/box/boxes`; the downloaded ASCII Box CLI also embeds `/api/box/boxes/...` routes.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer/operator handling because the blocker is external ASCII Box API validation and live credentialed proof, not a narrow local repair automation can safely complete.

target.title: feat: add ASCII Box provider
target.body:
## Summary

- add `provider: ascii-box` as a delegated [ASCII Box](https://box.ascii.dev) runner
- add ASCII Box config/env/flags, archive sync, run/warmup/list/status/stop, and provider docs
- document the provider in the README, provider index, and changelog

[ASCII Box](https://box.ascii.dev) is the Ubuntu sandbox VM service from
[ascii.dev](https://ascii.dev) (docker / GitHub CLI / common languages /
desktop preinstalled, EU-hosted on Hetzner, `box new|fork|stop|ssh|prompt`).
This wires it up as a delegated-run provider, mirroring the shape of
`provider: upstash-box` (PR #149) ã both are HTTP-API box services, so this
PR is a structural near-clone with `AsciiBoxConfig`, `https://ascii.dev` base
URL, and an `image` knob instead of `runtime`.

## Verification

- `go build ./...` ã clean
- `go test ./internal/providers/asciibox/... ./internal/providers/all/...` ã passes (the one local skip is `TestSyncWorkspaceCleansRemoteArchiveWhenExtractFails`, which needs `git` on `$PATH`; same constraint as `upstashbox`'s equivalent test)
- `go vet ./...` ã clean
- `gofmt` clean on all touched files

## Caveats for reviewer

I wired the REST shape from the same template as `upstashbox` (POST/GET `/api/box`, `/api/box/{id}/exec`, `/api/box/{id}/exec-stream`, `/api/box/{id}/files/upload`, `/api/box/{id}/files/write`, `Authorization: Bearer ãÎ`). The endpoint paths and auth header are best-guess based on REST conventions and the install script at `https://ascii.dev/api/box/install`. Before this is run live, the operator with ASCII Box CLI access should:

1. Verify the actual endpoint paths against the `box` CLI (e.g. by running `box new --debug` or checking the binary's strings).
2. Confirm `Authorization: Bearer <key>` is the correct auth shape (the `box` CLI installer doesn't reveal the auth header it uses).

If anything diverges, the change is local to `internal/providers/asciibox/client.go` ã public Crabbox surface stays the same.

## Config / Secrets

- reads `ASCII_BOX_API_KEY` or `CRABBOX_ASCII_BOX_API_KEY`
- no API key flags; no secrets stored in repo
- aliases: `ascii`, `asciibox`, `ascii-box`
target.author: zozo123
